etymology.description: Áô∂(Áô∂) (bŇć, "two outstretched feet") + śģ≥(śģ≥) (shŇę, "hand with stick") = beating or cutting grass -> later evolved into śí•(śí•) (bŇć, "to poke; to stir")
etymology.interpretation: The character ÁôĻ combines the imagery of feet and a hand with a stick, suggesting the action of moving or cutting grass, which aligns with an early use related to stirring or poking items.
